LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE NOV-22-2021 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GLOBUS MEDICAL INC.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT GLOBUS MEDICAL INC.'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT ("SAC") IS OVERRULED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/EPS)

LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE JUN-22-2022 HEARING RE: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD AS TO PLAINTIFF JAMES BABALLE, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH A MODIFICATION AS FOLLOWS: W. JAMES OSBORNE'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL IS GRANTED. PER THE AGREEMENT OF MR. OSBORNE AND THE PLAINTIFF, THE ORDER WILL BE IN EFFECT AS OF JULY 1, 2022. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. W. JAMES OSBORNE, Esq. of Law Office of James Osborne (Tel: 818-618-6535) as movant/counsel-of-record for plaintiff James Baballe;

HEARING RE: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD AS TO PLAINTIFF JAMES BABALLE.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification as follows: W. James Osborne's unopposed motion to be relieved as counsel is granted. Per the agreement of Mr. Osborne and the plaintiff, the order will be in effect as of July 1, 2022.

Order signed.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-09-2021 Continued To Jan-12-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-22-2021 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT GLOBUS MEDICAL INC.'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Globus Medical Inc.'s demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") is overruled.

Emergency Rule of Court 9 adopted by the Judicial Council provides that "the statutes of limitations and repose for civil causes of action that exceed 180 days are tolled from April 6, 2020, until October 1, 2020." (Cal. Rules of Court, App. I, Rule 9.) That rule tolled the two-year statute of limitations for personal injury actions in Code of Civil Procedure section 335.1. Tolling of statutes of limitations will "suspend or extend a statute of limitations . . . ." (McDonald v. Antelope Valley Community College Dist. (2008) 45 Cal.4th 88, 99.) Thus, even if Plaintiff's cause of action accrued as early as February 2019, when Defendant's medical orthopedic rod allegedly broke, Defendant does not show that Plaintiff's February 16, 2021 second amended complaint is time-barred.

Defendant presents no argument in its moving papers that the SAC fails to state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action and is fatally uncertain, and its attempt to do so for the first time on reply is improper. In any event, the Court rejects that contention. "[I]n testing a pleading against a demurrer the facts alleged in the pleading are deemed to be true however improbable they may be." (Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604.) Here, Plaintiff sufficiently alleges each cause of action in the second amended complaint. Moreover, Defendant asks the Court to resolve disputed issues of fact, such as the exact date of injury or accrual, that cannot be decided on demurrer. Lastly, a "demurrer for uncertainty is strictly construed, even where a complaint is in some respects uncertain, because ambiguities can be clarified under modern discovery procedures." (Khoury v. Maly's of California (1993) 14 Cal.App.4th 612, 616; see also Lickiss v. Financial Industry Regulatory Authority (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 1125, 1135 ["demurrers for uncertainty are disfavored, and are granted only if the pleading is so incomprehensible that a defendant cannot reasonably respond."]

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/EPS)
